leadText: Thunderbolt (Japanese: １０まんボルト 100,000 Volts) is a damage-dealing Electric-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TM24 in Generation I, from Generation III to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, and in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM36 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR08 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM126 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM067 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A. It can be upgraded to the special Z-Move Stoked Sparksurfer by Alolan Raichu holding Aloraichium Z, and to the special Z-Move 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt by Pikachu in a cap holding Pikashunium Z.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generation II to V 1.3 Generation VI onwards 1.4 Generation VIII 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.5 Generation IX 1.5.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] Thunderbolt does damage and has a 10% chance of paralyzing the target. Thunderbolt cannot paralyze Electric-type Pokémon. Generation II to V [ edit | edit source ] Thunderbolt can now paralyze Electric-type Pokémon. Thunderbolt can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Charge was used in the previous turn. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Thunderbolt's power was decreased from 95 to 90. It can no longer paralyze Electric-type Pokémon once again, due to them now being immune to paralysis . Thunderbolt can be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining an extra three appeal points if Charge was used in the prior turn. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Stad2 An Electric -type attack. Has a one-in-ten chance of paralyzing the target. G S C An attack that may cause paralysis. R S E Colo XD A strong electrical attack that may paralyze the foe. R S E (move) A strong electrical attack. It may paralyze the target. Colo XD (move) A powerful electric attack that may cause paralysis. (TM) FR LG A strong electrical attack that may also leave the foe paralyzed. D P Pt HG SS PBR A strong electric blast is loosed at the foe. It may also leave the foe paralyzed. B W B2 W2 A strong electric blast is loosed at the target. It may also leave the target with paralysis. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP A strong electric blast crashes down on the target. This may also leave the target with paralysis. LA S V The user attacks the target with a strong electric blast. This may also leave the target with paralysis. ZA The user attacks with a strong electric blast. This may also leave targets paralyzed.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Damages all foes around the user. It may also cause paralysis and prevent any attacks or moves. MD T D S Inflicts damage on all enemies within a 1-tile range. It may also afflict the enemies within a 1-tile range with a Paralysis status condition. A Pokémon with the Paralysis status has slowed movement and is incapable of action. B S L まわりの てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる しかも いっていのかくりつで まひじょうたいにかえる まひじょうたいのポケモンは わざがだせない Conq. A strong electric blast is loosed at the target. It may also leave the target with paralysis. MD GtI It damages nearby enemies. It could also cause the Paralysis status condition. With the Paralysis status, their Travel Speed goes down and they can't attack or use moves. S MD It damages nearby enemies. It could also cause paralysis. Monopoly: Pokémon Exclusive Kanto Edition Move all opponents to Zapdos, roll the dice, and no matter who owns it, they all pay you 4 times the amount rolled. Also, if Zapdos is unowned, you may buy it from the bank.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] This move's name is the same as the category of Electivire and Zebstrika . Thunderbolt is the only move that can be upgraded into multiple exclusive Z-Moves : Alolan Raichu 's Stoked Sparksurfer and Pikachu in a cap 's 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt . Thunderbolt's Japanese name, １０まんボルト 100,000 Volts , corresponds with the information present in some of Raichu's Pokédex entries , which state its electric bursts can reach and, in some cases, even exceed 100,000 volts. Several moves have Japanese names in the same format, referencing a number of volts. In Pokémon Adventures , Red 's Pikachu Pika used an improvised move called MegaVolt (Japanese: １００まんボルト 1,000,000 Volts ), whose measurement of 10 6 volts puts it at 10 times the strength of Thunderbolt. 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt (Japanese: １０００まんボルト 10,000,000 volts ), the exclusive Z-Move of Pikachu in a cap , has a measurement of 10 7 volts, being 100 times the voltage of Thunderbolt. Though not named in exactly the same format, Gigavolt Havoc 's (Japanese: スパーキングギガボルト Sparking Gigavolt ) name contains the metric prefix "giga", which indicates 10 9 (1,000,000,000), making it 10,000 times the voltage of Thunderbolt. While Thunderbolt is depicted crashing down on the opponent in the handheld games, its depiction in the console games is more akin to how it is depicted in the animated series, as the bolt goes straight toward the opponent. Despite this change, its description still stated "a strong electric blast crashes down on the target" until Pokémon Legends: Arceus . In Pokémon Red and Green , the battle effect for Thunderbolt contains several more, shorter flashes compared to the animation in the international Pokémon Red and Blue . The move animation was likely toned down to prevent epileptic seizures . In some copies of Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire , using Thunderbolt or Thunder may cause the game to freeze during the moves' animation sequences. This, however, can be prevented by turning off the battle effects in the options menu. In Pokémon FireRed, LeafGreen , and Emerald , the sound of Thunderbolt or Thunder may get stuck until the game resets. Because it has been a staple of Pikachu 's moveset since the fifth ever episode , Thunderbolt is one of the most, if not the most, featured move throughout all of Pokémon the Series . In addition, as revealed in Climbing the Walls! , it is Ash 's favorite move. Thunderbolt is among the three attacks enhanced into "new" moves in the Pokémon Adventures manga, the others being Razor Leaf and Thunder.
